two_body_problem.title: Optimal Initial Positioning
two_body_problem.principle: Place the red ball near the midpoint between the green and blue balls to minimize initial distance.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the red ball needs to initiate contact with the green ball and push it towards the blue ball.

two_body_problem.title: Adjust Vertical Placement for Contact Duration
two_body_problem.principle: Lower the red ball to ensure the green and blue balls remain in contact for at least 3 seconds.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the goal is to maintain contact duration between the green and blue balls.

two_body_problem.title: Balance Horizontal Offsets
two_body_problem.principle: Place the red ball closer to the blue ball to help bridge the gap between the green and blue balls.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the red ball needs to push the green ball towards the blue ball.

two_body_problem.title: Strategic Radius Selection
two_body_problem.principle: Choose a red ball radius that ensures contact duration of at least 3 seconds without causing overlap.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the red ball's radius affects contact duration and prevents overlap.

two_body_problem.title: Initial Red Ball Velocity Adjustment
two_body_problem.principle: Adjust the initial velocity of the red ball to ensure it initiates contact and maintains contact duration.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the initial velocity of the red ball impacts the contact initiation and duration.

two_body_problem.title: Horizontal Push Magnitude Control
two_body_problem.principle: Control the horizontal push magnitude by adjusting the red ball's initial velocity to ensure the green ball is pushed into the blue ball.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the horizontal push magnitude needs to be adjusted to ensure the green ball contacts the blue ball.

two_body_problem.title: Vertical Offset for Mid-Air Interaction
two_body_problem.principle: Place the red ball at a higher vertical position to allow for a mid-air interaction with the green ball.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the red ball needs to interact with the green ball in mid-air.

two_body_problem.title: Use Center of Blue Ball as Reference
two_body_problem.principle: Place the red ball relative to the center of the blue ball to ensure proper positioning.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the red ball needs to be positioned relative to the blue ball's center.

two_body_problem.title: Optimize Red Ball Radius for Contact Duration
two_body_problem.principle: Select a red ball radius that optimizes contact duration while avoiding overlap.
two_body_problem.when_to_apply: When the red ball's radius significantly affects contact duration and prevents overlap.

two_body_problem.description: Placing the red ball too far away horizontally from the green ball.
two_body_problem.why_it_happens: Agents may misjudge the required proximity for initiating contact.
two_body_problem.how_to_avoid: Ensure the red ball is placed near the midpoint between the green and blue balls.

two_body_problem.description: Not considering the vertical alignment of the balls when placing the red ball.
two_body_problem.why_it_happens: Agents may overlook the importance of vertical positioning for mid-air interactions.
two_body_problem.how_to_avoid: Place the red ball at a higher vertical position to allow for mid-air interactions.

two_body_problem.description: Incorrectly assuming the red ball will always push the green ball towards the blue ball regardless of its initial velocity.
two_body_problem.why_it_happens: Agents may underestimate the impact of initial velocity on the push direction.
two_body_problem.how_to_avoid: Adjust the initial velocity of the red ball to control the push direction.

two_body_problem.description: Incorrect radius selection leading to premature overlap or insufficient contact duration.
two_body_problem.why_it_happens: Agents may choose a radius that does not optimize contact duration.
two_body_problem.how_to_avoid: Select a red ball radius that ensures contact duration of at least 3 seconds without causing overlap.

two_body_problem.description: Red ball placed too high relative to the blue ball.
two_body_problem.why_it_happens: Agents may place the red ball too high, preventing mid-air interactions.
two_body_problem.how_to_avoid: Place the red ball at a higher vertical position to allow for mid-air interactions.
